CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(100), value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'first', 'value1');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (2, 'second', 'value2');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (3, 'third', NULL);
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 's%');
SELECT t1.id, t1.name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table t2 WHERE t2.name = t1.name) AS name_count FROM test_table t1;
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_table);
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE name = 'third' OR value IS NULL;
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table t3 WHERE t3.value = 'value1' AND t3.id = test_table.id);
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE value = (SELECT MIN(value) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL);
SELECT client_attr, (SELECT AVG(LENGTH(value)) FROM test_table) AS avg_length FROM test_table;
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table LEFT JOIN (SELECT id, COUNT(*) as count FROM test_table GROUP BY id) AS counts ON counts.id = test_table.id;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN status VARCHAR(50);
UPDATE test_table SET status = 'active' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT client_attr FROM test_table WHERE status = 'active' AND name IS NOT NULL;
